tools.name: investigate_phone
tools.description: Investigate an Indian mobile number and return who is behind it — name(s), addresses, alternate phone numbers, and social/digital footprint. Use depth='basic' for a fast identity bundle (default) or depth='full' for a comprehensive multi-source investigation (slower). Best for: 'who owns this number

tools.name: assess_fraud_risk
tools.description: Assess the fraud risk of an Indian mobile number. Returns a composite risk profile: SIM age / age-on-network, number revocation status, SIM-swap / port signals, and digital & financial exposure. Set detailed=true for the full signal breakdown. Best for: onboarding risk checks, is-this-number-risky.

tools.name: find_contacts
tools.description: Skip-trace an Indian mobile number for the person's ALTERNATE phone numbers and current, geocoded addresses. Requires the end user's consent: ask the user to open the consent link in portal.outris.com/mcp and pass the consent_token they receive. Best for: debt collection, locating a person. Never fa

tools.name: due_diligence_person_start
tools.description: Start a full due-diligence / background check on a PERSON, anchored on their mobile number (optionally add name, PAN, DOB, email, city). Covers PEP, sanctions, enforcement, cybercrime, breaches, directorships, and adverse media. PREMIUM and ASYNC (~40-70s): it returns a job_id — poll check_job until

tools.name: check_job
tools.description:
Check the status/result of an async job by job_id (e.g. from due_diligence_person_start). Free to poll — wait ~10s between polls.

Cost: 0 credits

tools.name: investigate_email
tools.description:
Trace the person behind an email address — linked names, phone numbers, addresses, and known data breaches. Best for: reverse email lookup, digital footprint, cross-referencing an identity.

Cost: 2 credits

tools.name: resolve_company
tools.description:
Resolve an Indian company from its NAME and return its CIN (Corporate Identification Number) plus any GSTIN / MSME registrations discovered along the way. Best for: 'is this a real company', KYB entry point, finding a CIN from a company name.

Cost: 3 credits

tools.name: lookup_gst
tools.description:
Look up GST registration details for a business by its GSTIN (15-character GST number). Returns legal/trade name, status, registration type, and address. Best for: GST verification, vendor onboarding.

Cost: 2 credits

tools.name: verify_pan
tools.description:
Verify an Indian PAN (Permanent Account Number) and return the holder's name, status, and PAN type (individual / company / etc.). Best for: KYC, PAN validity checks.

Cost: 2 credits

tools.name: lookup_vehicle
tools.description:
Look up an Indian vehicle by its RC (registration) number — returns make/model, registration details, and the registered owner. Best for: vehicle verification, RC checks.

Cost: 2 credits

tools.name: verify_bank_account
tools.description:
Validate an Indian bank account WITHOUT moving any money (no-debit NPCI validation) and return whether it is valid plus the account holder's name for matching. Best for: payout/beneficiary verification. This does NOT transfer funds.

Cost: 2 credits

tools.name: smart_lookup
tools.description: Answer any other identity / KYC / business question when no specific tool fits. Provide a natural-language `question` plus the `identifiers` you already have (phone, email, PAN, GSTIN, CIN, DIN, UAN, IFSC, vehicle RC, UPI VPA, UDIN, or a company name). It figures out the right lookup — or a short se

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "http" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: due_diligence_person_start, smart_lookup · Private data: find_contacts · Public sink: due_diligence_person_start, investigate_email, smart_lookup.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: due_diligence_person_start, smart_lookup · Private data: find_contacts · Public sink: due_diligence_person_start, investigate_email, smart_lookup

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
